info.title: Template API
info.description:
This API provides endpoints for managing application templates and instances in the Sealos platform.

## Authentication

Browsing templates is public. Deploying instances requires a URL-encoded kubeconfig in the `Authorization` header. Encode with `encodeURIComponent(kubeconfigYaml)` before setting the header value. Obtain your kubeconfig from the Sealos console.

## Errors

All error responses use a unified format:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "type": "validation_error",
    "code": "INVALID_PARAMETER",
    "message": "...",
    "details": [...]
  }
}
```

- `type` — high-level category (e.g. `validation_error`, `resource_error`, `internal_error`)
- `code` — stable identifier for programmatic handling
- `message` — human-readable explanation
- `details` — optional extra context; shape varies by `code` (field list, string, or object)

## Operations

**Query** (read-only): returns `200 OK` with data in the response body.

**Mutation** (write):
- Create → `201 Created` with the created resource in the response body.
- Update / Delete → `204 No Content` with no response body.

baseUrl.description: Base URL of your instance (e.g. https://template.192.168.x.x.nip.io)

tags.name: Query
tags.description: Read-only operations. Success: `200 OK` with data in the response body.

tags.name: Mutation
tags.description: Write operations. Create: `201 Created` with the new resource. Update/Delete: `204 No Content`.

get.description: Returns metadata only (no resource calculation). Response headers: `Cache-Control` (public, max-age=300, s-maxage=600), `ETag`. When categories exist, top category keys are returned in `X-Menu-Keys` (comma-separated). For full details including resource requirements, use `/templates/{name}`.

parameters.name: language

schema.description: Language code for internationalization (e.g., "en", "zh"). Defaults to "en"

parameters.description: Language code for internationalization (e.g., "en", "zh"). Defaults to "en"

X-Menu-Keys.description: Top category keys (comma-separated). Present only when categories exist.

readme.description: URL to README documentation. Empty string when not available.

icon.description: URL to template icon image. Empty string when not available.

gitRepo.description: Git repository URL. Empty string when not available.

deployCount.description: Number of deployments (includes multiplier for display)

schema.description: Array of template items (returned directly without wrapper object)

code.description: Specific error code for programmatic handling and i18n

details.description: Raw error string from the underlying system, for troubleshooting.

get.description: Returns complete template metadata with dynamically calculated resource requirements (CPU, memory, storage, NodePort count) derived from the template YAML. Falls back to static configuration if calculation fails. Response headers: `Cache-Control` (public, max-age=300, s-maxage=600), `ETag`.

parameters.name: language

schema.description: Language code for internationalization (e.g., "en", "zh"). Defaults to "en"

parameters.description: Language code for internationalization (e.g., "en", "zh"). Defaults to "en"

readme.description: URL to README documentation. Empty string when not available.

icon.description: URL to template icon image. Empty string when not available.

gitRepo.description: Git repository URL. Empty string when not available.

deployCount.description: Number of deployments (includes multiplier for display)

OPENAI_API_KEY.description: The API Key of the OpenAI-compatible service

400.description: Bad request - template name is required

code.description: Specific error code for programmatic handling and i18n

details.description: For INVALID_PARAMETER: Array<{ field, message }>. For INVALID_VALUE: optional string. Omitted for other codes.

field.description: Field path using dot/bracket notation, e.g. "ports[0].number"

message.description: Validation error message for this field

code.description: Specific error code for programmatic handling and i18n

details.description: Typically omitted. May contain additional context in edge cases.

code.description: Specific error code for programmatic handling and i18n

details.description: Raw error string from the underlying system, for troubleshooting.

post.description:
Deploy an arbitrary or custom template by supplying its raw YAML directly in the request body. The instance name is auto-generated from `${{ random(8) }}` inside `spec.defaults.app_name`. Use `dryRun: true` to validate the resources against the Kubernetes API without creating anything.

**Example — dry-run a custom template:**
```json
{
  "yaml": "apiVersion: app.sealos.io/v1\nkind: Template\n...",
  "dryRun": true
}
```

yaml.description: Full template YAML string. Must start with a `kind: Template` document, followed by one or more `---`-separated Kubernetes resource documents.
yaml.example:
apiVersion: app.sealos.io/v1
kind: Template
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  defaults:
    app_name:
      type: string
      value: my-app-${{ random(8) }}
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: ${{ defaults.app_name }}
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: ${{ defaults.app_name }}
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: ${{ defaults.app_name }}
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: nginx:latest
          resources:
            limits:
              cpu: 100m
              memory: 256Mi

args.description: Template variable key-value pairs that override or supply `spec.inputs` fields. Only args without a non-empty default are required.

dryRun.description: If true, validates the resources against the Kubernetes API but does not create anything. Returns 200 with a preview. Default: false.

200.description: Dry-run preview — resources validated but not created

name.description: Auto-generated instance name from ${{ random(8) }} in spec.defaults

args.description: Resolved template arguments after merging user-provided values with defaults

resources.description: Preview of sub-resources that would be created (Instance resource excluded)

name.description: Instance name (matches the name specified in the request)

uid.description: Kubernetes UID of the Instance resource

args.description: Resolved template arguments after merging user-provided values with defaults

400.description: Bad request — missing or invalid YAML / missing required args

code.description: Specific error code for programmatic handling and i18n

name.description: Instance name. 1–63 lowercase alphanumeric characters or hyphens, must start and end with alphanumeric (Kubernetes DNS subdomain rules). e.g. "my-perplexica-instance"

template.description: Template name from the catalog. Use GET /templates to list available templates. e.g. "perplexica"

args.description: Template variable key-value pairs. Only args without a default value are required. Use GET /templates/{name} to see which args are required and their defaults.

name.description: Instance name (matches the name specified in the request)

uid.description: Kubernetes UID of the Instance resource

kubeconfigAuth.name: Authorization
kubeconfigAuth.description: URL-encoded kubeconfig YAML. Encode with `encodeURIComponent(kubeconfigYaml)` before setting the header value. Obtain your kubeconfig from the Sealos console.
